title: Fix test_package_index test
body: It started to fail a few days ago, because of some extra terminal output.

files_modified.patch:
@@ -495,8 +495,8 @@ def test_package_index(tmp_path):
     assert result.returncode == 0
     stdout = re.sub(r"(?<=[<>=-])([\d+]\.?)+", "*", result.stdout)
     assert (
-        stdout.strip().rsplit("\n", 1)[-1]
-        == "Successfully installed attrs-* micropip-* numpy-* packaging-* sharedlib-test-py-*"
+        "Successfully installed attrs-* micropip-* numpy-* packaging-* sharedlib-test-py-*"
+        in stdout.strip().rsplit("\n", 1)[-1]
     )

steps.operation: Relax the assertion in the package index CLI test to tolerate extra terminal output by checking for substring presence instead of exact line equality
steps.reason: The test began failing due to additional terminal output appended to the last line; using an `in` check keeps the test focused on verifying the installation success message without being brittle to incidental output changes.
steps.side_effects: This reduces test strictness: if the output contains the expected success message alongside unexpected text, the test will still pass, potentially masking certain regressions in the exact formatting of the final output.

step_plan.summary: Updates the `test_package_index` assertion to be resilient to extra terminal output by asserting the success message is contained in the last stdout line rather than matching it exactly.
